inputs.label: TEMPERATURE
inputs.implementation: The DS18B20 module communicates over a single data wire using the 1-Wire protocol — just pull the data line high with a 4.7kΩ resistor and use any Dallas/OneWire library to request a conversion and read back the result. The bare DS18B20 IC works identically, just wire VDD, GND, and DQ the same way, keeping the pull-up resistor close to the pin.

inputs.label: LIGHT
inputs.implementation: Wire the BH1750 breakout to your microcontroller's I²C bus, set the ADDR pin low for address 0x23, and send a single measurement mode command before reading back the two-byte lux value. The VEML6030 IC is drop-in similar over I²C, but consult its register map to configure gain and integration time for your lighting conditions before reading the ALS output register.

inputs.label: MOTION
inputs.implementation: The HC-SR501 outputs a digital HIGH on its OUT pin when motion is detected. Power it at 5 V and connect OUT to any GPIO. Adjust sensitivity and hold-time with the two on-board trimpots.

inputs.label: SOUND LEVEL
inputs.implementation: The MAX4466 outputs an analog voltage proportional to sound amplitude — connect its OUT to an ADC pin. Adjust gain with the on-board trimpot. Keep power traces clean and away from digital lines to reduce noise.

inputs.label: MAGNETIC FIELD
inputs.implementation: QMC5883L module: Connect via I²C and use the QMC5883LCompass library to get calibrated heading and raw field strength on all three axes. QMC5883L (IC): Same I²C interface on the bare QFN chip — add 100nF decoupling caps and pull-ups on SDA/SCL, and calibrate for hard-iron offset in software.

inputs.label: MOISTURE
inputs.implementation: DHT11 module: Use the DHT library with a single data pin and read both temperature and humidity with one dht.read() call. SHT40A-AD1B-R2 (IC): Communicate over I²C using the fixed address 0x44, send a measure command, wait ~10ms, then read 6 bytes of raw temperature and humidity data.

inputs.label: ROTATION
inputs.implementation: AS5600 module: Place a diametrically magnetized magnet above the chip, connect via I²C, and read the 12-bit ANGLE register for absolute position. AS5600 (IC): Same register map on the bare SOIC package — ensure the magnet is centered axially within ~3mm for accurate readings.

inputs.label: ACCELERATION
inputs.implementation: ADXL345 module: Use the Adafruit ADXL345 library over I²C or SPI to get calibrated acceleration in m/s² on all three axes immediately. LIS2DH12 (IC): Initialize over I²C by setting the CTRL_REG1 register to enable axes and set ODR, then read 6 bytes from the output registers for X/Y/Z.

inputs.label: HEARTBEAT
inputs.implementation: MAX30102 module: Use the SparkFun MAX3010x library and run the provided heart-rate example sketch, which handles LED control and peak detection automatically. MAX30102 (IC): Same I²C interface on the bare die — configure LED pulse amplitude and sample rate via registers, then apply a peak-detection or Pan-Tompkins algorithm on the raw IR data in firmware.

outputs.label: MUSIC
outputs.implementation: Put a 300–500 Ω resistor in series on the data line close to the MCU. Add a 100–470 µF capacitor across VCC and GND near the first pixel. Power from 5 V; add a level-shifter if your MCU runs at 3.3 V.

outputs.label: BUZZER
outputs.implementation: Drive a passive buzzer with PWM through a 100 Ω series resistor — vary the frequency to change pitch. An active buzzer only needs a digital HIGH/LOW signal; it generates its own fixed tone internally.

outputs.label: BINARY
outputs.implementation: Connect 8 LEDs to 8 GPIO output pins, each through a 220–470 Ω current-limiting resistor. Represent the sensor reading in binary: bit 7 on the leftmost LED, bit 0 on the rightmost.

outputs.label: MORSE CODE
outputs.implementation: Blink a single GPIO output. Convention: dot = 100 ms ON, dash = 300 ms ON, gap between symbols = 100 ms OFF, gap between letters = 300 ms OFF. Encode your sensor value as a number in Morse.

outputs.label: LED MATRIX
outputs.implementation: The MAX7219 uses SPI (DIN, CS, CLK). On startup, configure the scan-limit register (0x0B) and intensity register (0x0A), then clear the display. Each row register (0x01–0x08) holds one byte of pixel data.

outputs.label: 7-SEGMENT DISPLAY
outputs.implementation: The TM1637 uses a 2-wire interface (CLK and DIO) — not standard I²C. Use a TM1637 library to display integers or floats directly. The module handles multiplexing internally; no extra resistors needed.

connectors.label: KEYCHAIN
connectors.implementation: Add two 3.2 mm drill holes (MountingHole_3.2mm_M3 footprint in KiCad) near one edge of the board, at least 3 mm from any copper trace. Loop a split ring or small carabiner through one hole after fabrication.

connectors.label: PUSHBUTTON
connectors.implementation: Wire one side of the button to a GPIO pin and the other side to GND. Enable the MCU's internal pull-up so the pin reads HIGH when open and LOW when pressed. Add a 20 ms software debounce to ignore contact bounce.

connectors.label: ROTARY KNOB
connectors.implementation: Connect the A and B pins to two GPIOs with 10 kΩ pull-ups (or enable internal pull-ups in firmware). Read the quadrature transitions to determine rotation direction and count. The EC11 also has a push-switch on SW/GND for click input.

connectors.label: TOGGLE SWITCH
connectors.implementation: Place the switch in series with your signal or power line, connecting one pad to the source and the other to the load, with a pull-down (or pull-up) resistor to keep the input pin at a defined logic level when the switch is open.

connectors.label: 3-WAY SWITCH
connectors.implementation: Route the traveler traces carefully to avoid accidental shorts, use an SW_SPDT footprint for each switch, and verify the logic with a truth table in your schematic before laying out the board since the crossing connections can be unintuitive.
